englishDefinitions: This appears to be a malformed or nonstandard phrase; it is not a common English expression.
If intended literally, it means to take a spoon in your hand.
examples: The phrase grab a the spoon is not a standard idiom in English.
He reached for the drawer to grab a the spoon, though the wording sounded unusual.
If you meant the literal action, just say 'grab a spoon.'
